sourceMovie.title: Wonder Woman
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: People revisit Wonder Woman for the same reason it broke through in 2017: clear stakes, memorable faces, and a adventure rhythm that doesn't stall in the middle. Patty Jenkins keeps the ensemble pointed in one direction. The action and adventure blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Wonder Woman 1984
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Where Wonder Woman excels in action, Wonder Woman 1984 (2020) doubles down on adventure to comparable effect. The tonal similarities run deep enough that watching both back-to-back feels intentional.

recommendations.title: Tomb Raider
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Tomb Raider (2018) is the natural next step after Wonder Woman: same action DNA, different perspective. The tense streak and adventure elevate it well above a simple genre exercise.

recommendations.title: Justice League: Gods and Monsters
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Fans of Wonder Woman who respond to its action will find Justice League: Gods and Monsters (2015) immediately familiar. Both films share a adventure sensibility without ever feeling like carbon copies of each other.

recommendations.title: Wonder Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: There's a reason Wonder Woman fans keep landing on Wonder Woman (2009) — the action is unmistakably related even when the plots diverge. Both films operate on the same emotional frequency, especially in their third acts.

recommendations.title: Green Lantern: First Flight
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Green Lantern: First Flight draws from the same action well as Wonder Woman, channeling adventure in ways that feel unmistakably related. The 2009 film makes a natural companion watch — similar in spirit but distinctly its own.

recommendations.title: Captain Marvel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: Captain Marvel (2019) captures the action appeal of Wonder Woman without simply imitating it. The adventure streak running through both films is what puts Captain Marvel at the top of any "if you liked…" shortlist.

recommendations.title: The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The action appeal of Wonder Woman — that tense edge, the adventure — translates cleanly into The Hunger Games: Mockingjay - Part 2 (2015). Different setting, same essential satisfaction.

recommendations.title: Alita: Battle Angel
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The filmmakers behind Alita: Battle Angel (2019) clearly understood what makes action work — the same quality that keeps Wonder Woman rewatchable. Expect the same tense rhythm, executed with a fresh angle.

recommendations.title: The Huntsman: Winter's War
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Huntsman: Winter's War (2016) is the kind of film Wonder Woman fans discover and immediately send to their group chat. The action overlap is real, but it's the tense emotional pull that seals the recommendation.

recommendations.title: The Fantastic 4: First Steps
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: The Fantastic 4: First Steps shares Wonder Woman's gift for action — the 2025 film delivers adventure without overstaying its welcome. Consider it required viewing once you've finished rewatching your first pick.

faq.question: What should I watch after Wonder Woman?
faq.answer: Try Wonder Woman 1984, Tomb Raider, Justice League: Gods and Monsters—each shares DNA with Wonder Woman while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Wonder Woman?
faq.answer: Wonder Woman (2017) is best described as Action, Adventure, Fantasy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Wonder Woman part of a franchise?
faq.answer: Yes—it's part of the "Wonder Woman Collection" storyline. You can enjoy it standalone, but sequels and tie-ins build on characters and continuity established here.
